intuition: If any element appears more than once, a set will have fewer elements than the array. Alternatively, insert elements into a set and check for duplicates during insertion.
approach: Create a hash set. Iterate through the array — for each element, check if it's already in the set. If yes, return true. Otherwise, add it. If we finish without finding a duplicate, return false.
walkthrough: i=0: 1 not in set. Add 1. Set: {1}
i=1: 2 not in set. Add 2. Set: {1, 2}
i=2: 3 not in set. Add 3. Set: {1, 2, 3}
i=3: 1 IS in set → return true

complexity.space: O(n) — set stores up to n elements

hints: What data structure allows O(1) lookup for 'have I seen this before?'
You could sort first (O(n log n)) and check adjacent elements, but hashing is faster.
Alternatively, compare set size vs array length.
commonMistakes: Sorting the array and forgetting that it modifies the original — use a copy if needed.
Using nested loops for O(n²) — a set makes it O(n).
author: chron303
